---
title: "Kb Rmd test"
output:
  md_document:
    pandoc_args: ["--wrap=none"]
    variant: gfm
    preserve_yaml: TRUE
knit: (function(inputFile, encoding) {
  rmarkdown::render(inputFile, encoding = encoding, output_dir = "../_posts") })
author: "Kulbhushan"
date: "16/02/2021"
excerpt: "Here are two tips for having what I think will be an easier blogging experience in R Markdown and Jekyll."
layout: single
categories:
  - R Markdown
  - Jekyll
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE, cache=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(stringr)
library(here)
# get name of file during knitting and strip file extension
rmd_filename <- str_remove(knitr::current_input(), "\\.Rmd")

# Figure path on disk = base.dir + fig.path
# Figure URL online = base.url + fig.path
knitr::opts_knit$set(base.dir = str_c(here::here(),'/'), base.url= '/') # project root folder
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
	fig.path = str_c("assets/images/posts/", rmd_filename, '/'),
	message = FALSE,
	warning = FALSE,
	cache.path = "../cache/",
	dev = "svg",
	dpi = 300
)
```

Some footnote. [^1]

some citation [^2]. 




# Initialization

```{r echo=TRUE, message=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(gt)
library(formatR)
```

```{r message=FALSE}
#font_import()
#loadfonts(device = "win")
windowsFonts(Times = windowsFont("TT Times New Roman"))
```

```{r}
theme_set(theme_classic())

# Plot
g <- ggplot(mpg, aes(cty))
g + geom_density(aes(fill=factor(cyl)), alpha=0.8) + 
    labs(title="Density plot", 
         subtitle="City Mileage Grouped by Number of cylinders",
         caption="Source: mpg",
         x="City Mileage",
         fill="# Cylinders")
```

## References

[^1]: This is the foot note at the end.

[^2]: hello 2nd citation
